title: Shear wave velocity and Poisson's ratio models across the southern Chile convergent margin at 38°15′S
abstract: A shear (S) wave velocity model and a Poisson's ratio model are derived along a profile at 38°15′S across the Chilean convergent margin at the site of the 1960 Mw 9.5 Valdivia earthquake. In the upper part of the profile, controlled source data from explosive shots used in the TIPTEQ Seismic experiment were processed for S-wave arrivals. The results show that seismic velocities and Poisson's ratios in the continental crust are consistent with geological features inferred from other studies.

location.description: TIPTEQ seismic profile at 38°15'S across the 1960 Valdivia earthquake rupture zone, south-central Chile

data.name: TIPTEQ OBS/Land Seismometer S-Wave Data — 38°15'S
data.description: Amphibious wide-angle seismic data from TIPTEQ SO181 at 38°15'S used to derive S-wave velocity model and Poisson's ratio across the Valdivia earthquake zone; explosive shots used as sources for S-wave analysis. [TIPTEQ cruise (SO181, 2004/2005) datasets searchable on PANGAEA]

data.name: TIPTEQ Swath Bathymetry — South-Central Chile
data.description: Multibeam bathymetry from SO181 TIPTEQ cruise along the south-central Chile margin. [TIPTEQ cruise (SO181, 2004/2005) datasets searchable on PANGAEA]

_data_note: TIPTEQ SO181 data accessible via MGDS. Amphibious experiment with OBS in marine portion and land stations onshore.

processing.summary: Controlled-source seismic refraction data from the TIPTEQ SO181 amphibious experiment at 38°15'S were reprocessed specifically for S-wave arrivals generated by explosive shots. Forward modeling and tomographic inversion yield 2D Vs and Vp/Vs (Poisson's ratio) models across the Chilean convergent margin at the 1960 Valdivia earthquake site. Results show Poisson's ratios consistent with geological constraints from previous Vp studies.
workflow: 1. OBS/land station data extraction and instrument correction
2. S-wave arrival time picking (Sg, Sn, SmS)
processing.notes: Uses existing TIPTEQ NVR dataset reprocessed for S-wave arrivals; companion to Groß et al. 2008 reflection study

analysis_notes: GJI paper; behind paywall. TIPTEQ SO181 data accessible via MGDS. Uses same dataset as Groß et al. 2008 and Ramos et al. 2018.
